definition.en: The soul or entity is always in a state of flux, or learning, and of developments that have to do with subjective experience rather than with time or space. This is not nearly as mysterious as it might sound. Each of my readers plays a game in which the egotistical conscious self pretends not to know what the whole self definitely does know. Since the ego is definitely a part of the whole self, then it must necessarily be basically aware of such knowledge. In its intense focus in physical reality, however, it pretends not to know, until it feels able to utilize the information in physical terms.
definition.zh: 灵魂或存有始终处于流动、学习与发展的状态，这些发展关乎主观体验，而非时间或空间。这并不像听上去那般神秘。我的每一位读者都在玩一场游戏：自我中心的意识自我假装不知道整体自我确知的一切。既然自我无疑是整体自我的一部分，那么它必然在根本上知晓这些知识。然而，当它全神贯注于物质实相时，便会假装不知，直到它感觉能够以物质形式运用这些信息为止。

definition.en: In certain terms, the ego is the eye through which the conscious mind perceives, or the focus through which it views physical reality. But the conscious mind automatically changes its focus throughout life. The ego, while appearing the same to itself, ever changes. It is only when the conscious mind becomes rigid in its direction, or allows the ego to take on some of its own functions, that difficulties arise. Then the ego allows the conscious mind to work in certain directions and blocks its awareness in others.
definition.zh: 从某种角度来说，自我是意识心用以感知的眼睛，或是它聚焦于物质实相的焦点。但意识心在一生中会自动改变其焦点。自我虽然自认为始终如一，实则不断变化。只有当意识心变得僵化固执，或允许自我接管部分自身功能时，困难才会产生。此时自我会允许意识心在特定方向运作，却阻碍其觉察其他方向。

definition.en: (Pause at 10:43.) Now often the ego acts as a dam, to hold back other perceptions -- not because it was meant to, or because it is in the nature of an ego to behave in such a fashion, or even because it is a main function of an ego, but simply because you have been taught that the purpose of an ego is restrictive rather than expanding. You actually imagine that the ego is a very weak portion of the self, that it must defend itself against other areas of the self that are far stronger and more persuasive and indeed more dangerous; and so you have trained it to wear blinders, and quite against its natural inclinations.
definition.zh: （在10:43停顿）现在，自我常常像一道堤坝，阻挡其他感知——但这并非其本意，也不是自我的本性使然，甚至不是自我的主要功能，而仅仅是因为你们被教导说，自我的目的是限制而非扩展。你们实际上认为自我是自我中非常脆弱的部分，必须防御自我中其他更强大、更有说服力、甚至更危险的部分；因此你们训练它戴上眼罩，这完全违背了它的自然倾向。

definition.en: (10:13.) Many probable systems have perceptive mechanisms far different from your own. In fact, some are based upon gestalts of awareness completely alien to you. Quite without realizing it, your ego is a result of group consciousness, for example; the one consciousness that most directly faces the exterior world, is dependent upon the minute consciousness that resides within each living cell of your body; and as a rule you are only aware of one ego -- at least at a time.
definition.zh: （10点13分）许多可能性系统的感知机制与你们截然不同。事实上，有些系统建立在与你们完全陌生的意识完形之上。举例而言，你们的自我（ego）正是群体意识的结果——那个最直接面对外在世界的单一意识，实则依赖于你们身体每个活细胞内所栖息的细微意识。通常来说，你们只能觉察到一个自我——至少在同一时间内如此。

definition.en: The ego is a very specialized portion of your greater identity. It is a portion of you that arises to deal directly with the life that the larger You is living. The ego can feel cut off, lonely and frightened, however, if the conscious mind lets the ego run away with it. The ego and the conscious mind are not the same thing. The ego is composed of various portions of the personality -- it is a combination of characteristics, ever-changing, that act in unitary fashion -- the portion of the personality that deals most directly with the world.
definition.zh: 自我是你更大身份中一个非常专门化的部分。它是你的一部分，专门处理那个更大的你所正在经历的生活。然而，如果意识心让自我牵着鼻子走，自我就会感到孤立、孤独和恐惧。自我和意识心并非同一回事。自我由人格的各个不同部分所组成——它是各种特性的组合，不断变化，却以统一的方式运作——是人格中最直接处理外界事务的那一部分。

explanation:
自我（Ego）是人格中高度专门化的部分，它从更大的整体身份中浮现，以直接处理个体在物质世界中的生活。自我并非整个自我，而是自我意识的一种特定状态——在这种状态中，意识试图与行动分离，将行动视为客体，并误以为行动是由自我发起的，而非自我存在的根源。外在自我是内在自我的仿冒形象，它主要关注物理领域的接触、发展与进步，是面向外部的自我表面，如同坐在房子前廊的看管者，而内在自我则负责维护整个自体的基础与平衡，接收来自整体存有的讯息。

外在自我与内在自我之间存在深刻的分隔。外在自我通常不愿承认内在自我的存在，如同眼睛无法不借助镜子看见自己的瞳孔。内在自我通过内在感官接收讯息，知晓外在自我每一步行动、每一口呼吸、每一个梦境，是人格的源头和存有的代表。外在自我则受限于物理场域，其意识聚焦于瞬间点，被时间概念所束缚——它恐惧过去（因已失去控制）与未来（因尚未掌控），却正是时间概念的创造者。

自我具有强烈的稳定性与永久性追求，它试图变得僵化，否认内在情绪的变化性，因为情绪的可变性威胁到它自认为的永久性。当自我过于狭窄、未能充分代表潜意识中仍存有的从属人格时，这些从属人格会在条件有利时通过自我瞬间的弱点表达自身。自我拒绝接纳的行动或情绪会形成被排斥的冲动模式，这些模式通过吸引力聚集，并试图不顾自我的限制而寻求表达。自我的力量实际上取决于它灵活接纳与同化日益复杂行动的能力，而非僵化地拒绝它们。

值得注意的是，自我并非必然存在，但某种类型的自我始终是个体性的基础。内在自我作为经验的组织者，在内在实相中运作并创造外在自我所处理的物理实相。随着存在层次的提升，内外自我开始融合，内在实相的直接经验通过潜意识的“嘴巴”被“勺喂”给外在自我。人类对意识自我的迷恋导致忽视了使自我成为可能的那部分自己，忽视了赋予自我其引以为傲力量的根源。

quotes.text: Because of its nature ego does not want to adjust. It wants adjustments to be made to it. Because ego is another manifestation of action, it is of course impossible for its aims to be realized. For all its attempts at stability and control, ego itself constantly changes. ego most of all resents and fights against time as you know it, yet ego is to a large extent responsible for your conception of time. Basically, ego fears both the past and the present. It fears the past because it has already lost control of the past. It fears the future because it is not yet in control of it. It seeks continuity of identity, yet it is forced to realize that the "I" of today is hardly the "I" of thirty years ago.
quotes.translation: 由于自我的本质，它不愿调整，而要求外界向它调整。因为自我是行动的另一显化，其目标自然无法实现。尽管自我竭力追求稳定与控制，它自身却不断变化。自我最憎恨并抗拒你们所认知的时间，然而在很大程度上，正是自我造就了你们的时间观念。从根本上说，自我既恐惧过去也恐惧现在——它恐惧过去是因为已失去对它的掌控，恐惧未来则因尚未能掌控。它寻求身份的连续性，却被迫意识到今日之"我"与三十年前的"我"已判若两人。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: (From the 242nd session for March 16, 1966:) The ego is not the most powerful or the most knowledgeable portion of the self. It is simply a well-specialized part of the personality, fully equipped to operate under certain circumstances21 ... When those conditions no longer exist [after "death"], then other layers of the self take over the dominant position, and the personality realigns its psychological components. The ego does not disappear, however; it merely takes a back seat in some respects, as your own subconscious does during physical existence. The ego is under the control of what may loosely be called "the inner self." The survival or nonphysical personality has somewhat the same relationship to the ego as the dreaming personality has to it in physical life.
quotes.translation: （摘自1966年3月16日第242节：）自我并非自身最强大或最博知的部分。它只是人格中高度专门化的部分，完全装备好在特定环境下运作……当这些条件在“死后”不复存在时，自身的其他层次便占据主导地位，人格重新调整其心理组成。然而自我并未消失；它只是在某些方面退居次要位置，如同你们肉身存在期间潜意识所处的状态。自我受控于可粗略称为“内在自己”的部分。存续或非肉身人格与自我的关系，某种程度上如同做梦人格在肉身生活中与自我的关系。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The inner ego knows when to apply safety valves, and is aware of the danger before the outer ego is alerted. The inner ego is concerned with maintaining the foundations and balance, which is very important, of the whole self, and it is open to messages from the overall entity. The inner ego receives messages through the inner senses, and is aware of realities which the outer ego cannot afford to recognize because of its specialization. In some important aspects the outer ego is supposed to represent to some degree the subdominant personalities who still dwell in the subconscious. When the outer ego is narrow, and poorly represents these subdominant personalities then they rise up in arms, and when conditions are favorable attempt to express themselves through a momentary weakness on the part of the dominant ego. But without even doing this they may momentarily take over or express themselves through a single function, such as speech or motion, while the outer ego is blissfully unaware.
quotes.translation: 内在自我知道何时应用安全阀，并在外在自我警觉之前就意识到危险。内在自我关注维持整个自身的基础与平衡——这非常重要——并且它向整体存有的信息敞开。内在自我通过内在感官接收信息，并意识到那些因自身专门化而无法承认的实相。在某些重要方面，外在自我本应在一定程度上代表仍居于潜意识的次主导人格。当外在自我狭隘且未能充分代表这些次主导人格时，它们便会奋起反抗，并在条件有利时试图通过主导自我的短暂弱点来表达自己。但即使不这样做，它们也可能在某个单一功能（如言语或动作）上暂时接管或表达自己，而外在自我却浑然不觉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: As far as self and not-self are concerned, the unit of self is organized, as you know, by the inner ego, which directs the whole energy field. The outer ego directs the manipulation of this gestalt in the physical universe. The outer ego is rather more tied to physical properties, and yet it can directly experience inner reality by a change of focus through aligning itself with the inner ego, focusing its energies with the inner rather than the outer senses. This is by far the most advantageous method of experiencing inner reality, because the outer ego is therefore consciously aware of what has been going on, and can use such knowledge in its own sphere.
quotes.translation: 关于自我与非我的分野，自我单元是由内在自我组织的——它统御着整个能量场。而外在自我则负责在物质宇宙中操控这个完形结构。外在自我虽更受限于物质属性，却可通过调整焦点与内在自我对齐，将能量聚焦于内感官而非外感官，从而直接体验内在实相。这是体验内在实相最有利的方式，因为外在自我能清醒觉知所发生的一切，并在其领域内善用这些知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: At times the ego can hold you in a tight vice, which the dissociation breaks. This is what happened after your exercises. You have been doing very well, for you, in allowing yourself psychic freedom. However conscious fears cause the ego to tighten its grasp and some effects of this nature were starting up again. This is why I suggested that you begin these exercises now. The fact that the fearful ego was beginning to tighten explains your reaction to the exercises. The ego can build up around the subconscious vitality like a glacier, and these exercises melt it away. Even the prickles in your neck are like tiny picks chipping away at icy fears.
quotes.translation: 有时自我会如铁钳般紧锁你，而解离状态能打破这种束缚。这正是你练习后发生的情况。就你而言，你在允许自己获得心灵自由方面已做得相当出色。然而意识层面的恐惧会使自我收紧掌控，这类性质的效应又开始重现。这就是我建议你现在开始这些练习的原因。恐惧的自我开始收紧的事实，解释了你对练习的反应。自我会像冰川般在潜意识活力周围堆积，而这些练习能将其消融。就连你颈部的刺痛感，都如同细小的冰镐在凿碎冰封的恐惧。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The subconscious, and in fact all portions of the self with the exception of the ego, are capable of assimilating a wider area, so to speak, of action. Therefore to these other portions of the self, time has a much different essence than it has for the ego. The ego is indeed many things. It can be defined in relationship to many other aspects of reality. In relationship to action, and moment points, the ego is indeed that portion of the self which stands at the apex of the moment point, and is limited by the moment point. The ego is in this context the portion of the self which is utterly focused upon, and imprisoned by, the moment point.
quotes.translation: 潜意识，事实上除了自我之外的所有自我部分，可以说都能吸收更广阔的行动领域。因此对这些自我部分而言，时间具有与自我截然不同的本质。自我确实是多重面向的存在。它可以根据与实相其他许多方面的关系来定义。就行动与瞬间点而言，自我确实是立于瞬间点顶端、受限于瞬间点的自我部分。在此脉络下，自我是完全聚焦于瞬间点、并被瞬间点囚禁的自我部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The rejected emotion, in other words, will express itself in any case, but it will do so then as a rebel, outside of the organizational directives of the ego itself. Hence for example, actions that appear senseless to the ego are often the results of such unassimilated or denied emotions. At one time or another such emotions were acceptable to the ego. There was an attraction, or the emotion would not have been permitted to enter into a realm close to ego control.
quotes.translation: 被拒绝的情感无论如何都会表达自己，但届时会以叛逆者的姿态出现，脱离自我本身的组织指令。举例来说，自我看似毫无意义的行动，往往就是这类未被整合或否认的情感所致。这些情感在某个时刻曾为自我所接纳。若非存在吸引力，这类情感根本不会被允许进入接近自我控制的领域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Two main possibilities come to mind. One, a continued lack of acceptance of the inner self could lead to worldwide catastrophe, as the ego runs wild. Two, the inner self, restrained and denied for too long a period, could explosively overwhelm the ego so that existence in the physical field was made most difficult, since the ego is equipped to handle such manipulation. ego domination must indeed be halted, but it must be replaced with a balance between the outer and inner selves. Other matters briefly mentioned this evening will be more thoroughly discussed then also.
quotes.translation: 我想到了两种主要可能性。其一，持续拒绝接纳内在自体可能导致全球性灾难，因为自我会失控。其二，长期被压抑和否定的内在自体可能以爆发性方式压倒自我，使得物质世界的存在变得极为困难——因为自我本应处理这类操控。自我的支配确实必须被制止，但必须以内外自体间的平衡来取代。今晚简要提及的其他事项届时也将更详尽讨论。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The time sequence varies also as you have seen. That is, events are experienced in different sequences. However past, present and future has reality only to the ego. Now there is some bleed-through. The ego is certainly aware to some extent of the subconscious. The subconscious is certainly aware of the ego. The portion of the self, or rather the portions of the self, that experience action in terms of what the ego would term probability, is further divorced from the ego, however, and this probability reality appears to the ego very rarely, and only very occasionally.
quotes.translation: 如你所见，时间序列也会变化。也就是说，事件以不同顺序被体验。然而过去、现在和未来仅对自我具有真实性。现在存在一些渗透现象。自我确实在一定程度上觉察到潜意识。潜意识当然也觉察到自我。但体验以自我所谓"可能性"形式运作的自体部分（或更确切地说，自体诸部分）则与自我更加疏离，这种可能性实相极少且仅偶尔显现于自我面前。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: In some cases of course the secondary personality would be a severely impeding one, stealing away the main energies. However, it must be understood that from the whole personality's viewpoint, a strong ego, that is a dependable one, and one that will also allow necessary expression, is a necessity. Therefore from the standpoint of the whole personality, the adoption of a new ego, so to speak, with a more practical grouping of unifying principles, could be the best solution of the previous ego, the previously dominant ego, （if it） had been an incapable one.
quotes.translation: 当然在某些案例中，次人格可能成为严重阻碍者，窃取主要能量。但必须理解：从整体人格的视角来看，一个强大的自我——即可靠且允许必要表达的自我——是必需的。因此就整体人格而言，采用一个具有更实用统一原则组合的新自我（姑且如此说），可能是解决先前那个无能的、先前主导性自我的最佳方案。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: It is only the ego that steps from moment to moment, as a man who walks from puddle to puddle. It is only the ego who drowns in time. Therefore, since only the ego is momentarily imprisoned within the focus of your plane, it is only the ego who probes so slowly into simultaneous action, perceiving it bit by bit and sip by sip. So now you will see what I meant when I spoke about the limitless self, for the whole self is not so bound. The whole self could and does perceive a limitless number of such moment points simultaneously. And now hear this:
quotes.translation: 只有自我才会从一个瞬间跨到另一个瞬间，如同一个人从一滩水洼跨到另一滩水洼。只有自我才会溺毙于时间之中。因此，既然只有自我暂时囚禁于你们层面的焦点内，也只有自我才会如此缓慢地探索同时性行动，一点一滴地感知它。现在你们会明白，当我谈到无限自性时是什么意思——因为整个自性并不受此束缚。整个自性能且确实同时感知无数个这样的瞬间点。现在请听好：
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The ego is a very specialized portion of your greater identity. It is a portion of you that arises to deal directly with the life that the larger You is living. The ego can feel cut off, lonely and frightened, however, if the conscious mind lets the ego run away with it. The ego and the conscious mind are not the same thing. The ego is composed of various portions of the personality -- it is a combination of characteristics, ever-changing, that act in unitary fashion -- the portion of the personality that deals most directly with the world.
quotes.translation: 自我是你更大身份的一个非常专门化的部分。它是你的一部分，浮现出来直接处理那个更大的你所经历的生活。然而，如果意识心让自我牵着鼻子走，自我会感到被切断、孤独和恐惧。自我和意识心不是同一回事。自我由人格的各种部分构成——它是不断变化的特质组合，以统一的方式运作——是人格中最直接与外界打交道的部分。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: The peculiar and individualistic aspects of personality are the result of those camouflaging abilities of which we have spoken earlier. Those portions of the personality which escape ego's attempts to dominate are held suspect in egos eyes. ego considers them as invalid and dangerous to its own supremacy. When ego is forced to admit that personality changes, it will do its best to avoid this knowledge. The more rigid an ego is, the more danger there is that the individual will have difficulties in all kinds of adjustments.
quotes.translation: 人格中那些独特而个人化的方面，是我们之前谈到的伪装能力的结果。人格中那些逃脱自我控制的部分，在自我眼中被视为可疑。自我认为它们无效且对其至高无上性构成威胁。当自我被迫承认人格会改变时，它会尽力回避这个认知。自我越僵化，个体在各种调适上遇到困难的风险就越大。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: These sub personalities are not unconscious to themselves. They are conscious of themselves, but they are not conscious of themselves in relation to other selves. They are conscious of needs and drives, and of their existence. They are different from the inner ego or director in that the inner ego is conscious not only of itself, but of the outer ego, and is aware of the existence of the outer world, although not too much concerned with it unless the whole self becomes jeopardized through the actions of the outer ego.
quotes.translation: 这些次人格对自身并非无意识。它们意识到自身的存在，但并未意识到自身与其他自我之间的关系。它们意识到自身的需求、驱动力以及存在。它们与内在自我或导演的不同之处在于，内在自我不仅意识到自身，也意识到外在自我，并且知晓外在世界的存在——尽管除非整个自我因外在自我的行为而陷入危险，否则它并不会过分关注外在世界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: In certain terms, the ego is the eye through which the conscious mind perceives, or the focus through which it views physical reality. But the conscious mind automatically changes its focus throughout life. The ego, while appearing the same to itself, ever changes. It is only when the conscious mind becomes rigid in its direction, or allows the ego to take on some of its own functions, that difficulties arise. Then the ego allows the conscious mind to work in certain directions and blocks its awareness in others.
quotes.translation: 从某种角度而言，自我是意识心用以感知的眼睛，或是它聚焦物质实相的焦点。但意识心终其一生都在自动改变其焦点。自我虽然看似始终如一，实则不断变化。只有当意识心变得僵化固着，或允许自我接管其部分功能时，困难才会出现。此时自我允许意识心朝特定方向运作，却阻断了它在其他方向的觉知。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: What you have here is an attempt to objectify, or stand apart from action in such a refusal. This is not the fault of the subconscious, but a fault of the ego, which refuses to assimilate or accept a given action. As you know, it is the ego who exists as a result of such objectivity. All the qualities that make up the ego are objectified to that degree, but they are collected about the ego with the ego as center. When the ego however refuses to accept an emotion as a part of itself, it tries one of two actions.
quotes.translation: 在此你看到的是试图将行动客观化，或通过这种拒绝与行动保持距离的尝试。这并非潜意识的过错，而是自我的过失——它拒绝同化或接纳某个既定行动。如你所知，正是这种客观化造就了自我的存在。构成自我的所有特质都以此方式被客观化，但它们以自我为中心聚集。然而当自我拒绝接纳某个情绪作为自身的一部分时，它会尝试两种行动之一。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The personal subconscious and the ego are indeed equal partners in the formation of any given present personality. Ruburt's results are evidence that his training is bearing fruit. Once such a relationship is set up between the ego and the subconscious, then communication in general between the two will always improve. The subconscious, even the personal subconscious, is much freer from the moment point than is the ego, and it can inform the ego of important developments which can be of great help.
quotes.translation: 个人潜意识与自我确实是形成任何特定当前人格的平等伙伴。鲁柏的结果证明他的训练正在结出果实。一旦自我与潜意识之间建立起这样的关系，两者之间的整体沟通便会持续改善。潜意识，甚至个人潜意识，比自我更不受当下时刻的束缚，它能将重要的发展告知自我，这对自我大有裨益。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Many reactions, many patterns of reactions, are rejected by the ego upon some occasions and accepted upon other occasions, but as a rule such alternate behavior is annoying to the ego itself. The ego deals with cause and effect, and often denies particular reactions because it decides that they are not effective. The ego is fairly rigid, comparatively speaking. Rationalization is one method by which the ego justifies its acceptance of a reaction which it once rejected as ineffective.
quotes.translation: 许多反应、许多反应模式，有时被自我拒绝，有时又被接受，但通常这种交替行为会让自我本身感到困扰。自我处理因果关系，常常否认特定反应，因为它断定这些反应无效。相对而言，自我相当僵化。合理化是自我为其接受一种曾被视为无效的反应而辩护的一种方法。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: In the various levels of existence the inner and outer egos begin to merge. Gradually direct experience of inner reality is spoon-fed by the inner ego through the mouth of the subconscious to the outer ego. The division between the inner and outer egos is necessary for other reasons than the nature of direct experience itself. That is, the outer ego is shielded from direct experience of reality because it could not take the impact of such experience. But this is not the only reason.
quotes.translation: 在存在的各个层次中，内在自我与外在自我开始融合。逐渐地，内在自我通过潜意识之口，将内在实相的直接体验一勺勺喂给外在自我。内在自我与外在自我之间的划分，除了直接体验本身的性质外，还有其他原因。也就是说，外在自我被屏蔽于实相的直接体验之外，因为它无法承受这种体验的冲击。但这并非唯一的原因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Many limitations are set upon personalities by their own entities for karmic reasons. I want to make it plain that free will does exist, but that it is limited through use of a more extended free will on the part of the entity. Also, the inner ego is aware of so-called future decisions not because he forces such decisions upon the outer ego but simply because the future as such does not exist to the inner ego, and therefore he can perceive where the outer ego cannot.
quotes.translation: 许多限制是由人格自身的本体基于业力原因设定的。我想明确说明：自由意志确实存在，但受到本体层面更扩展自由意志的制约。同时，内在自我之所以知晓所谓的未来决定，并非因为它将此类决定强加于外在自我，而仅仅是因为对内在自我而言，未来本身并不存在，因此它能看见外在自我无法看见的领域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Tuesday does not exist to the inner ego. Your outer ego is now experiencing the camouflage time year of 1964. This simply does not exist to the inner ego, therefore the inner ego is effortlessly aware of any choices or decisions that you may make freely in say 1970. It does not affect your choice. It has absolutely nothing to do with whatever future choice you may make. It is aware of such future choices simply because the camouflage future does not exist for it.
quotes.translation: 星期二对内在自我而言并不存在。你的外在自我此刻正经历着伪装时间年1964年。这对内在自我而言根本不存在，因此内在自我能毫不费力地感知你在1970年可能自由做出的任何选择或决定。它不影响你的选择，与你未来可能做出的任何决定毫无关联。它之所以知晓这些未来选择，仅仅因为伪装未来对它而言并不存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The ego does not exist within the electrical field. The ego is a product of the physical field, formed from physical birth on. The inner identity and individuality, as you know, has its origins long before this. The inner self adopts an ego in order to allow manipulation within the physical universe, and yet part of the ego is composed of portions from the inner self, while the bulk of the ego is allowed to develop through physical heredity and environment.
quotes.translation: 自我并不存在于电场中。自我是物理场的产物，自肉体诞生起形成。如你所知，内在身份与个体性早在此时之前便已存在。内在自体为了在物质宇宙中运作而采纳了一个自我，但自我的一部分由内在自体成分构成，而自我的主体部分则通过物质遗传与环境得以发展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: This inner ego knows the outer ego well. The outer ego is but a counterfeit image of the inner ego. The outer ego as a rule is not aware of what you may think of for now as the thoughts or communications of the inner ego; but the inner ego knows every step you take, every particle of air you breathe, every dream you have; and it is the source of your own personality and is the representative of the entity of which it is part.
quotes.translation: 这个内在自我非常了解外在自我。外在自我不过是内在自我的一个虚假影像。外在自我通常并不觉察你此刻可能认为的内在自我的思想或沟通；但内在自我知道你走的每一步、你呼吸的每一粒空气、你做的每一个梦；它是你人格的源头，也是它所隶属的存有的代表。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: (Faster at 10:45:) The god concept then was an aid, and an important one, to man's emerging ego. To develop its sense of specialization, the ego forgot the great cooperative venture of the earth. If a hunter literally knows his relationship with an animal, he cannot kill it. On deeper levels both animal and man understand the connections. Biologically the man knows he has come from the earth. Some of his cells have been the cells of animals, and the animal knows he will look out through a man's eyes.3 The earth venture is cooperative. The slain beast is tomorrow's hunter. In terms of ego consciousness, however, there were stages of growth; and the god concepts that spoke of oneness with nature were not those that served the ego's purposes in the line of development as you understand it (deliberately).
quotes.translation: （10:45加快语速：）因此，神的概念对人类正在浮现的自我来说，是一个重要且必要的辅助。为了发展其专门化的感知，自我遗忘了地球的伟大合作事业。如果一个猎人真正了解他与动物的关系，他就无法杀死它。在更深的层面上，动物和人类都理解这些连结。生物层面上，人知道自己来自地球。他的一些细胞曾是动物的细胞，而动物也知道自己将通过人的眼睛向外看。地球的事业是合作的。被宰杀的野兽是明天的猎人。然而，就自我意识而言，存在不同的成长阶段；那些谈论与自然合一的"神"概念，并不符合你所理解的自我在发展路线上的目的（刻意强调）。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In other words, portions of the inner self have joined the strictly egotistical functions. The ego in such cases is so attuned that it becomes almost something else. We are coming here toward a definition of illumination in psychic terms. The ego is not, underlined, banished. It joins with portions of the inner self previously unconscious, and illuminates the whole personality. It is no longer primarily physically oriented. Therefore it is no longer an ego in the terms usually meant.
quotes.translation: 换句话说，内在自体的部分已经加入了严格以自我为中心的功能。在这种情况下，自我如此调谐，以至于它几乎变成了别的东西。我们在此正接近一个灵性术语上的"开悟"定义。自我并没有被驱除（强调）。它与先前无意识的内在自体部分结合，并照亮了整个的人格。它不再主要导向物质层面。因此，它不再是通常意义上的自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Without these chemical and electromagnetic connections, （voice louder） the ego as such would not exist. The ego is largely dependent upon the physical mechanisms that compose the corporal image. It has its beginnings with corporal birth. While we speak of the ego, this part of the self is hardly permanent or constant, but ever-changing. However the intellect is also a portion, or an attribute, of the ego.
quotes.translation: 没有这些化学与电磁连接（提高音量），自我本身便不会存在。自我很大程度上依赖于构成肉体形象的物质机制。它始于肉体诞生之时。当我们谈论自我时，自我的这一部分并非恒常不变，而是不断变化的。然而，智力也是自我的一部分或一种属性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Barriers are cut away, and once you become used to receiving within a larger range you will be able to do so in your future. These ranges, once opened up, then do not entirely close up to you. It becomes easier for you in your future to receive within them. A disciplined but intuitive ego then becomes a necessity in order that such new experience may be brought under useful control, in your (underlined) terms. The ego become more necessary you see, not less, for it must learn to synthesize the gained experience in recognizable terms within your system. Otherwise if the ego is pushed aside it will rise up in arms and in opposition, and can be highly dangerous.
quotes.translation: 屏障被消除，一旦你习惯于在更广的范围内接收，未来你便能做到这一点。这些范围一旦开启，就不会完全对你关闭。未来你更容易在其中接收。一个受纪律约束但直觉敏锐的自我变得必要，以便这些新体验能在你们（强调）的术语下得到有用的控制。你看，自我变得更重要，而非更不重要，因为它必须学会在你们的系统内以可识别的术语综合所获得的体验。否则，如果自我被推到一边，它会奋起反抗，可能非常危险。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: The outer ego is very seldom aware of the inner ego, and the subconscious is indeed a vast area dividing them. We are discussing now the outer ego in relation to the inner ego, and describing a situation in terms of relationships. Other relationships would show both the outer and inner egos in a different light. Relationships are also the result of tensions, and each action sets up a new tension.
quotes.translation: 外在自我很少意识到内在自我，而潜意识确实是分隔它们的广阔领域。我们现在讨论的是外在自我与内在自我的关系，并以关系的角度描述一种情境。其他关系会从不同角度展现外在自我与内在自我。关系也是张力的结果，每个行动都会建立新的张力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Consciousness of self involves a consciousness of self within, amid, and as a part of action. ego consciousness, on the other hand, involves a state in which consciousness of self attempts to divorce itself from action, an attempt on the part of consciousness to perceive action as an object. Here we see that ego consciousness, in this attempt, strives to perceive action not only as separate, but to perceive it in such a fashion that it appears to ego that action is not only separate from itself, that is separate from the ego, but that action is initiated by the ego, and a result rather than a cause of ego's own existence.
quotes.translation: 自我意识涉及对内在自我的觉知，这个自我处于行动之中、作为行动的一部分。而另一方面，自我意识则涉及一种状态，其中自我意识试图与行动分离，是意识试图将行动视为客体的尝试。在此我们看到，自我意识在这种尝试中，不仅力求将行动视为分离的，而且要以这样的方式感知行动：使行动在自我看来不仅与自身分离——即与自我分离——而且行动是由自我发起的，是自我自身存在的结果而非原因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The four faces of Eve all represented various ego manifestations of one inner identity. The course of the ego is a precarious one, and any number of potential egos exist within any identity. The Three Faces of Eve is an excellent title for the book, since the ego may quite legitimately be compared to the face that the identity turns toward objective reality, or the living mask that it dons.
quotes.translation: 夏娃的四张面孔都代表了同一个内在身份的各种自我表现。自我的历程是危险的，任何身份中都存在着无数潜在自我。《夏娃的三张面孔》是这本书的绝佳书名，因为自我完全可以被比作身份转向客观现实时所展现的面孔，或是它戴上的活面具。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Even if they are recalled as dreams however, they may appear meaningless, for they are unfamiliar to the ego. Yesterday's events reenacted in a dream touch off familiarity. Tomorrow's events in tonight's dreams do not, not at least to the ego. Generic codes apply in other words to the future as well as to the past, but mankind does not generally perceive them as such for they appear meaningless to the ego, because of the ego's inherent nature and limitations.
quotes.translation: 即使这些事件以梦境形式被忆起，它们仍可能显得毫无意义，因为自我对此感到陌生。昨日事件在梦中重演能引发熟悉感，而今夜梦境中的明日事件则不会——至少对自我而言不会。换言之，通用编码既适用于过去也适用于未来，但人类通常无法如此认知，因为受限于自我固有的本质与局限，这些编码对自我而言毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: In many respects it is a reflector, the surface of the self looking outward. While this is necessary, the whole remainder of the self could not be left to an organizer or caretaker who did not focus his attentions within the depths but sat, as it were, on the front porch of the house, leaving the inner workings unattended. The outer ego does not want to meet the inner ego. The outer ego does not want to admit the existence of the inner ego. As the eye cannot see its own pupil without a mirror, so the outer ego could not even see itself, were it not that the inner ego hides in the depths of all reflections.
quotes.translation: 从许多方面来看，自我是一面反射镜，是面向外在的自我表面。虽然这是必要的，但自我的其余部分不能交由一位不专注内在深处、反而如同坐在房屋前廊般忽视内在运作的管家或照料者来管理。外在自我不愿遇见内在自我，外在自我不愿承认内在自我的存在。正如没有镜子眼睛便无法看见自己的瞳孔，若非内在自我隐匿于所有反射的深处，外在自我甚至无法看见自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The word slow, or slowly, is not good in this connotation, since of itself it does not necessarily suggest safety, but a mere neutral and temporary putting off. So far Ruburt is doing well, and will learn as he progresses how to operate inner acceleration and flow, or rush of energy, so that he is comfortable. And the ego, through experience, will learn that the inner self will always return safely. And it would be most unjust, and needlessly cruel, not to give the ego this necessary reassurance; and also the inner self should manage to communicate to the ego its gratitude for the egos protective concern.
quotes.translation: "缓慢"或"慢速"一词在此语境中并不恰当，因其本身未必暗示安全，仅代表一种中性的暂时搁置。目前鲁柏进展良好，随着修行深入，他将学会如何运作内在加速与能量流或能量奔涌，从而感到舒适。而自我将通过经验明白，内在自我总会安全归来。若不给予自我这份必要的安心，甚至不让内在自我设法向自我表达对其保护性关注的感激，那将是极不公正且无谓残忍的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The subconscious however is not so limited. If you consider the ego at the apex of the moment point, and imprisoned therefore within the realm of its own before-and-after, cause-and-effect experience, then you can imagine the subconscious reaching further outward and seizing upon many other moment points. It should be easy to see then why the focus of the ego is so sharp and brilliant. Within its limited scope there is intensity of stimuli and response. Indeed, the ego is that portion of the personality which is plunged into a specific and intense preoccupation with a given field of action or dimension.
quotes.translation: 然而潜意识并不受此限制。若你将自我视为当下时刻的顶点，因而被囚禁于其自身的前因后果、因果体验领域之中，那么你可以想象潜意识向外延伸得更远，捕捉到许多其他时刻点。由此便不难理解为何自我的焦点如此锐利而鲜明。在其有限的范围内，刺激与反应具有强烈的强度。事实上，自我正是人格中那个投身于特定行动场域或维度、并与之产生强烈专注的那一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: But identity is much more than this, and basic identity, while using the perceptive abilities, is not that dependent upon them. It is true that the personality is a gestalt, and that every identity has any number of potential egos. It is also true that on occasion one potential ego will take over from another. But this is all highly simplified, for the ego structure is not one thing, but a changing, never constant, actually quite informal grouping of psychological patterns. Each ego uses and interprets the organisms perceiving apparatus in a way that in the overall is characteristic and distinctive.
quotes.translation: 但身份认同远不止于此，而基本身份认同虽运用感知能力，却并不如此依赖它们。诚然，人格是一个完形，每个身份认同都拥有无数潜在的自我。在某些情况下，一个潜在自我确实会接替另一个。但这已是高度简化的说法，因为自我结构并非单一实体，而是一组不断变化、从未恒定、实际上相当非正式的心理模式集合。每个自我都以整体上具有特征性和独特性的方式运用并诠释有机体的感知装置。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: This ego in particular, and many egos, consider that the self is the ego alone. The ego considers that therefore it must maintain stability and permanence. It therefore attempts to become rigid, because it considers itself the main representative of the self. It attempts to deny the inner emotions because the changeability of these emotions would seem to threaten its own permanence. It does not want to change. Therefore any seemingly small incident will tend to bring forth the explosion of these emotions quite against the egos inclination, precisely because the ego denies them so vehemently.
quotes.translation: 这个特定的自我，以及许多自我，都认为自我仅仅就是自我本身。自我因此认为它必须维持稳定与恒常。它试图变得僵化，因为它视自己为自我的主要代表。它试图否认内在情绪，因为这些情绪的多变性似乎会威胁其自身的恒常性。它不愿改变。因此任何看似微小的事件往往会引发这些情绪的爆发，完全违背自我的意愿，恰恰是因为自我如此激烈地否认它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Such alternative reactions frighten the ego because they seem to injure the ego's self-image. Yet all characteristic reactions, whether denied by the ego or not, are kept for use as alternative actions. In many cases actions unacceptable to the ego may be precisely those actions that are necessary for whole other areas of the personality. When too many actions are restricted by the ego, they may begin to form impulse patterns or groupings of various rejected impulses. These then adhere through attraction, and attempt to find expression regardless of the ego's attempts to restrict expression.
quotes.translation: 这类替代性反应会让自我感到恐惧，因为它们似乎损害了自我的自我形象。然而所有特征性反应，无论是否被自我否认，都会被保留下来作为替代行动。在许多情况下，不被自我接纳的行动，恰恰可能是人格其他完整领域所必需的行动。当太多行动受到自我限制时，它们可能开始形成冲动模式或各种被拒绝冲动的集合。这些冲动随后通过吸引力相互依附，并试图不顾自我限制表达的企图而寻求表达。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: You know that this is distortive. You recognize elements from the past, since your ego is familiar with them. You accept them into the organization of your perception on a conscious level, usually. The ego does not recognize elements of the future when they do appear within dreams, and it does not therefore admit them into perceptive patterns. The ego does not perceive their significance. It is only for this reason that certain events seem to be always in your future: this lack of recognition, identification, acceptance and organization into patterns that can be used and manipulated.
quotes.translation: 你知道这是扭曲的。你识别出过去的元素，因为你的自我对它们很熟悉。你通常会在意识层面将它们接纳到你的感知组织中。当未来的元素确实出现在梦境中时，自我无法识别它们，因此也不会将它们纳入感知模式。自我无法感知它们的重要性。正因如此，某些事件似乎永远存在于你的未来中：这种缺乏识别、确认、接纳以及组织成可被利用和操控模式的状态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: It was necessary for the ego, in its origins, to objectify itself as much as possible. Now however the stability of the ego, generally speaking, as a part of the human personality is established. It can now afford to be much more elastic, to include, in other words, more and more of reality within its awareness. Such an inclusion would be most beneficial. It would of course however to some extent change the ego, and any change is resisted by the ego.
quotes.translation: 自我在其起源时，有必要尽可能地将自身客体化。然而现在，一般而言，自我作为人类人格一部分的稳定性已经确立。它现在可以变得更有弹性，换言之，将越来越多的实相纳入其觉知中。这样的纳入将极为有益。当然，这会在某种程度上改变自我，而任何改变都会遭到自我的抗拒。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The ego is the only part of the self that regards physical objects as anything but symbols. It is highly difficult for other parts of the self to experience the ego for this reason. The ego, while always changing, is one of the most rigid aspects of identity. To the inner self neither house nor walls exist. They are perceived only as vague self limiting ideas on the ego's part.
quotes.translation: 自我是自体唯一将物质对象视为非象征的部分。正因如此，自体的其他部分极难体验自我。自我虽不断变化，却是身份认同中最僵化的面向之一。对内在自体而言，房屋与墙壁皆不存在，它们仅被感知为自我部分模糊的自我限制概念。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: The survival personality therefore momentarily inserts his ego in its old position. It can then interpret this data in terms that can be received and understood. This reassembly however does cause some disorientation on the part of the survival personality. The ego, back in its dominant position, finds the circumstances difficult to deal with. It is being asked to manipulate in a manner unfamiliar to it. The survival personality's inner self gives this reassembled ego concepts in the same way that, often, the subconscious gives the ego concepts in physical existence.
quotes.translation: 因此生存人格会暂时将自我插回其原有位置。如此便能以可接收理解的方式诠释这些数据。然而这种重组确实会造成生存人格的定向障碍。重返主导地位的自我发现处境难以应对，它被要求以不熟悉的方式运作。生存人格的内在自体向这个重组后的自我传递概念，其方式正如物质实相中潜意识常向自我传递概念一般。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Inclinations with which the ego has very little liking, for example, are very seldom a problem for the ego, since they remain generally outside of the ego pattern, never having been chosen by it to form a characteristic part of the ego pattern. Obviously, to some degree every conceivable sort of inclination is latent to the ego, but it is apparent that each ego has its peculiar set of adopted characteristics, its set of characteristics that it sometimes accepts and sometimes rejects; and it is obvious that some characteristics simply seem alien to any given ego.
quotes.translation: 例如自我极不喜好的倾向很少成为其困扰，因这些倾向通常停留在自我模式之外，从未被自我选择为特征性组成部分。显然，每种可想象的倾向在某种程度上都潜藏于自我之中，但每个自我显然都有其独特采纳的特质组合，有某些时而接受时而拒绝的特质；更明显的是，某些特质对特定自我而言根本就是格格不入。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Now the inner ego is the organizer of experience that Jung would call unconscious. The inner ego is another term for what we call the inner self. As the outer ego manipulates within the environment and physical reality, so the inner ego or self organizes and manipulates within an inner reality. The inner ego creates that physical reality with which the outer ego then deals.
quotes.translation: 内在自我是荣格称为无意识的经验组织者。内在自我是我们所称的内在自性的另一个术语。如同外在自我在环境与物质实相中运作，内在自我或自性也在内在实相中组织与运作。内在自我创造了外在自我随后要处理的那个物质实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The ego, as you now know it. will not always be necessary, you see, but a type of ego is a basis for individuality and will always be necessary. An inner ego, of course, that contains various egos that have been or will be a portion of any given self--these egos and any ego organizes experience along various lines and ties experience together within meaningful pattern.
quotes.translation: 你现在所知的自我，你看，并非永远必要，但某种形式的自我是个体性的基础，将永远必要。当然，内在自我包含曾属于或将成为任何特定自性一部分的多个自我——这些自我及任何自我都沿着不同路线组织经验，并将经验串联成有意义的模式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: So should man's ego be. When man's ego turns instead into a shell, when instead of interpreting outside conditions it reacts too violently against them, then it hardens, becomes an imprisoning form that begins to snuff out important data, and to keep enlarging information from the inner self. The purpose of the ego is protective. It is also a device to enable the inner self to inhabit the physical plane. It is in other words a camouflage.
quotes.translation: 人类的自我也应如此。当人类的自我反而变成硬壳，当它非但不去诠释外界状况，反而过度激烈地对抗它们时，它就会硬化，变成一种禁锢形态，开始扼杀重要资料，并持续阻隔来自内在自性的扩展信息。自我的目的是保护性的。它也是让内在自性得以栖身物质层面的装置。换言之，它是一种伪装。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The health and psychic condition of an individual is not primarily determined by the ego, however. It is only when the ego is allowed too much power that the individual is deprived of much of the inner vitality of the whole self. For the ego is acquainted with only its role. It can find refreshment only within the limitations of the reality which it was formed to meet; and when it looks about with the best of intentions and sees disasters and terrors, it does not know that these others also play their roles, and that the roles are temporary.
quotes.translation: 然而，个体的健康与心灵状态并非主要由自我决定。只有当自我被赋予过多力量时，个体才会被剥夺整体自性的大量内在活力。因为自我只熟悉它自己的角色，只能在它被塑造来应对的现实局限中找到慰藉；当它怀着最佳意图环顾四周，目睹灾难与恐惧时，它并不知道这些角色也各有其扮演，且角色都是暂时的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: When you are only operating within physical reality, you have a fairly simple set of rules to serve you. Within dream reality you are much freer. The ego is not present. The waking consciousness, dear friends, is not the ego. The ego is merely a small portion of waking consciousness. The ego is the portion of waking consciousness that deals with physical manipulation.
quotes.translation: 当你只在物质现实中运作时，有一套相当简单的规则可供遵循。在梦境现实中，你自由得多。自我并不在场。亲爱的朋友们，清醒意识并非自我。自我只是清醒意识的一小部分，是处理物质操控的那部分清醒意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Through experience in the various levels of existence the inner ego and the outer ego come closer and closer together. The subconscious eventually disappears, as it is no longer needed as a necessary buffer zone. At your stage of development the inner ego is by far the most self-conscious part of the whole self, and has the greatest ability for perception and organization. It alone is capable of experiencing inner and basic reality directly and immediately. It alone can cope with the tumultuous nature of such direct reality experience.
quotes.translation: 通过在不同存在层面的体验，内在自我与外在自我逐渐趋近。潜意识最终消失，因为它不再需要作为必要的缓冲地带。在你们的发展阶段，内在自我无疑是整体自性中最具自我意识的部分，拥有最强的感知与组织能力。唯有它能直接且即时地体验内在与基本实相，唯有它能应对这种直接实相体验的汹涌本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The fact is that the whole self is constantly experiencing data from all of the inner senses. The inner ego is of course aware of this. The subconscious is sometimes aware of this, and the outer ego is aware of very little. I have explained the reasons for this in past sessions. The outer ego must concentrate much of its energy toward survival in and manipulation of the outer camouflage world. This world has already been created by the inner self, and its continuing existence is determined by the constant vigilance of the inner self.
quotes.translation: 事实是，整个自己持续接收来自所有内在感官的数据。内在自我当然知晓这一点。潜意识有时也知晓，而外在自我则所知甚少。我已在过去的课程中解释过原因。外在自我必须将大部分能量集中于外在伪装世界的生存与操控上。这个世界早已被内在自己创造，其持续存在取决于内在自己的持续警觉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: When the outer ego, from the surface of its consciousness, reflects the outer world, it sees reflections of the inner ego which are the images within its own eye; and as the self creates matter subconsciously within its own eye, and as the self creates matter subconsciously and not consciously, and as the self creates matter in line with inner and not outer expectations, so then does the ego, in viewing the material universe, come face to face with the face of its own inner ego; and the outer ego cannot escape from this inner self.
quotes.translation: 当外在自我从其意识表层映照外在世界时，它看见的是内在自我的倒影——那些存在于它自身眼中的影像；正如自己潜意识地在自身眼中创造物质，正如自己以潜意识而非显意识的方式创造物质，正如自己依照内在而非外在的期望创造物质——因此，当自我审视物质宇宙时，它直面的是自己内在自我的面容；外在自我无法逃离这个内在自己。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: It may be necessary in the beginning to induce more or less complete amnesia, but this should be avoided. Through constant practice with hypnotism your scientists will discover that it is possible, and definitely beneficial, to allow the conscious ego to retain its memory. Since there is no real division between the ego and the subconscious, the knowledge of the ego and the knowledge of the subconscious are merged in any case. But to be truly effective there should be no attempt to hide subconscious knowledge from the ego.
quotes.translation: 起初或许需要诱发近乎完全的失忆，但这应当避免。通过持续练习催眠术，你们的科学家将会发现：让显意识自我保留记忆是可能的，且绝对有益。由于自我与潜意识之间并无真实分野，自我的知识与潜意识的知识无论如何都会融合。但若要真正有效，就不应试图对自我隐瞒潜意识的知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: What I am trying to get across here is that, because of the superficial studies done so far, it seems that telepathy operates with separateness because of the limitations set by the ego on the definition of self. It is the similarity, or sympathy, of those in telepathic communication that makes telepathy possible. The limited idea of the self held by the ego clouds and distorts the main issue, for the ego is not conscious of the underlying bonds of sympathy that serve to merge, momentarily, the identities involved.
quotes.translation: 我在此试图说明的是，由于迄今所做的肤浅研究，似乎心电感应因自我对自我定义的限制而运作于分离状态。正是心电感应沟通者之间的相似性或共鸣，才使心电感应成为可能。自我所持有的有限自我观念遮蔽并扭曲了主要问题，因为自我并未意识到那些暂时融合相关身份的潜在共鸣纽带。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The inner self and the deeper layers of the subconscious are relatively free however from the egos false gods, and it is for this reason that precognitions are at all possible. For these layers of the self are simply able to perceive a larger portion of action than the ego is able or willing to perceive. As a rule, the ego will not even accept the information that is derived in such a manner, for to do so would be to deny those artificial precepts of continuity upon which it feels its dominance rests.
quotes.translation: 然而，内在自我与潜意识较深层次相对而言摆脱了自我的虚假神祇，正因如此，预知才完全可能。因为这些自我层次能够比自我所能或愿意感知的，感知到更大范围的活动。通常，自我甚至不会接受以这种方式获得的信息，因为这样做将否定那些它认为其支配地位所依赖的人为连续性教条。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: But as it is not wise to dispense with the idea of nationalism without gradual growths of understanding and preparation, and while the idea of nationalism cannot suddenly be dispensed with, so also the ego cannot be, and will not be, overthrown overnight; and even when it is finally left behind, it will still be used as a handy reference point; and through all this the self will not lose but gain, for all expansion outward, and expansion inward is a gain, and all boundaries, whether inward or outward, are hampering and limiting. Basically, the self is not limited. The self does not need imaginary fences to protect its privacy, or its safety or its solitude. Only the ego is afraid of challenge, and therefore speaks of such limiting safety.
quotes.translation: 但正如在没有逐步的理解和准备下，摒弃国家主义观念是不明智的，且国家主义观念不能突然被抛弃，同样地，自我也不能且不会在一夜之间被推翻；即使最终被超越，它仍将被用作方便的参考点；而在此过程中，自我不会失去反而会获得，因为所有向外扩展和向内扩展都是获得，而所有边界，无论向内还是向外，都是阻碍和限制。基本上，自我不受限制。自我不需要想象的围栏来保护其隐私、安全或孤独。只有自我害怕挑战，因此才谈论这种限制性的安全。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The combined molecular consciousnesses, retaining identity, form a gestalt consciousness that is the ego, the outer ego, that is in turn utilized by the consciousness of the inner ego. The inner ego being unhampered by the laws of your plane, once having entered your plane at physical birth, can therefore leave the physical body and then reconstruct it.
quotes.translation: 组合的分子意识，在保持身份的同时，形成一个完形意识，即自我，外在自我，而内在自我的意识则利用这个外在自我。内在自我不受你们层面法则的束缚，一旦在物质出生时进入你们的层面，便能离开物质身体并重新构建它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Egos concept of itself as a rock of unchanging identity is highly ludicrous, since its perceptions constantly change it from what it was. It is not a thing proceeding, by any means. This is not to deny the human aspects of the ego, for the ego is also somewhat like a light carried in front of the inner self, a light that gives meaning to the physical universe and to its objects. It enables the inner self to manipulate within physical reality. It translates outer data to the inner personality.
quotes.translation: 自我将自己视为一块永恒不变的身份之石的概念极为荒谬，因为它的感知不断改变它，使它不再是原来的样子。它绝非一个按部就班前进的事物。这并非否认自我的人类面向，因为自我也像一盏提在内我前方的灯，一盏赋予物质宇宙及其物体意义的灯。它使内我能在物质实相中运作，并将外在数据翻译给内在人格。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: You cannot probe into the one system without affecting the others. It is basically meaningless to consider the personality as separate from the simultaneous actions of which it is composed. The personality, as you know, has also a reality within the dream universe. It should be obvious that in a most basic and practical manner the personality however is not involved with physical time to any degree. Only a part of the personality, the ego, is so involved. It is obvious of course that the personality system will react to stimuli that seem, to the ego, to be far divorced in time. That is, the personality may react to a stimuli in the present that occurred originally twenty years ago, to the ego's understanding of time.
quotes.translation: 你无法在不影响其他系统的情况下探究其中一个系统。将人格视为与其所构成的同步行动相分离，基本上毫无意义。如你所知，人格在梦境宇宙中也具有实相。显而易见，在最基本且实际的方式中，人格无论如何都不涉及物质时间。只有人格的一部分——自我——才如此涉及。当然，人格系统会对那些在自我看来时间上相隔甚远的刺激做出反应。也就是说，人格可能会在当下对二十年前最初发生的刺激做出反应，而这在自我对时间的理解中是如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: A note now concerning thought, as it is a form of action with which all men are familiar. Here you can see that your ego accepts thoughts as a part of its identity. Thought's actions are accepted by the ego, yet the ego seems to stand apart from them; and because of ego's nature it fears to plunge into the action of a thought. For it, the ego, has but recently pried itself from action, and so perceives action now as if action were a province of the ego, and not the other way around.
quotes.translation: 现在谈一下关于思想的问题，因为思想是所有人都熟悉的一种行动形式。在此你可以看到，你的自我将思想视为其身份的一部分。自我接受思想的行动，却又似乎与它们保持距离；由于自我的本性，它害怕投入思想的行动中。因为自我刚刚才从行动中挣脱出来，所以现在它将行动视为自我的领域，而非相反。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The inner ego is indeed the "I" of your dreams, having somewhat the same sort of position within the inner self as the ego itself has to the physical self. Actions however merge within the inner ego. The scope of awareness is more complex. We will at a later time discuss this inner ego in connection with the dream situation and health.
quotes.translation: 内在自我确实是你梦境中的"我"，它在内在自我中的地位，类似于自我在物质自我中的地位。然而，行动在内在自我中融合。觉知的范围更为复杂。我们稍后将讨论这个内在自我与梦境情境及健康的关系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: This is the ideal circumstance, for when this is the case then the ego listens to the inner self, and then directs its energy outward in a way that is beneficial for the whole gestalt framework. What you call negative suggestions are often judgments of the ego, for suggestion works from the ego to the subconscious, as well as it works the other way around. Such suggestions made by the ego can indeed be caught, and positive or constructive suggestions given to replace them.
quotes.translation: 这是理想的情况，因为当这种情况发生时，自我会倾听内在自我，然后以有益于整体完形架构的方式向外引导其能量。你所谓的负面暗示往往是自我的判断，因为暗示既从自我作用于潜意识，也以相反的方式运作。自我所做的这类暗示确实可以被捕捉到，并用正面或建设性的暗示来取代它们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: As you have an ego, fully conscious, directed toward the physical world, you also have what I call an inner ego, directed toward inner reality. You have, in other words, a portion of yourself that is fully conscious in Framework 2. The ego in your ordinary world, which again we will call Framework 1, is uniquely equipped to deal with that environment. It manipulates with rules of cause and effect and consecutive moments. It deals with an objectified reality. It can stretch its capacities, becoming far more aware of inner events than it is normally allowed to do, but its main purpose is to deal with the world of effects, to encounter events.
quotes.translation: 正如你们拥有一个完全清醒、面向物质世界的自我，你们也拥有我所说的内在自我，它面向内在实相。换句话说，你们有一部分自己在框架二中完全清醒。你们普通世界中的自我（我们再次称之为框架一）被独特地装备以处理那个环境。它依据因果律和连续时刻运作，处理客观化的实相。它可以扩展其能力，比通常允许的程度更深刻地觉察内在事件，但其主要目的是处理结果的世界，遭遇事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The ego, as I have said before, is extremely important. It is however only a part of what you call yourself. Your artistic ability does not belong to your ego, dear Joseph, though I understand as I speak that anyone on your plane identifies himself with his ego, and when he says "I am," he means he is his ego, or his ego is.
quotes.translation: 正如我之前所说，自我极其重要。然而，它只是你们所谓自我的一部分。亲爱的约瑟夫，你的艺术才能并不属于你的自我，尽管我明白，在我说话时，你们层面上的任何人都将自己等同于他的自我，当他说"我是"时，他意指他就是他的自我，或他的自我就是。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The ego is indeed a necessity within the physical field at this point of mans development. The ego is in a state of becoming, however. The ego is not what it was centuries ago, and it will not be the same centuries from now. It, the ego, will not admit the change, but its refusal to admit change in no way stops change.
quotes.translation: 在人类发展的现阶段，自我在物质领域内确实是必要的。然而，自我处于一种"成为"的状态。自我并非几个世纪前的样子，几个世纪后它也不会是现在的样子。它——自我——不会承认改变，但它拒绝承认改变绝无法阻止改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: We have in the past discussed in full the manner in which physical matter is formed. We have also discussed the ego, and our friend would do well to read the sessions dealing with the ego, and the ego's relationship to action. For one of the basic reasons for the difficulty lies in the fact that the ego of the present personality does strongly attempt to stand apart from action. It attempts to force action, and to fight action which it does not initiate.
quotes.translation: 我们过去已详尽讨论过物质形态的形成方式。也探讨过自我，我们的朋友最好重读那些涉及自我及其与行动关系的章节。因为困境的根本原因之一，在于当前人格的自我确实强烈试图脱离行动。它试图强迫行动，并对抗非由它发起的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The solution therefore may not necessarily be accepted completely as the best solution by the ego. The ego is aware of the physical situation only in its relationship to itself. It is not aware of the internal situation. The solution however must be made in relation to the total conditions. It is extremely important then that the ego correctly interprets the physical situation, for this is the information that it will give to the computer, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 因此，解决方案未必会被自我完全接受为最佳方案。自我仅在与自身相关的层面认知物理情境，却对内在情境毫无觉察。然而解决方案必须基于整体状况来制定。因此，让自我正确解读物理情境至关重要——这好比是它将输入计算机的信息。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: In other words the ego is not familiar with the probable self, but certain portions of the subconscious are. For the subconscious, like the probable self, is aware of its existence in the inverted time system. The subconscious is aware of many realities which are not accepted by the ego as actual physical events, and it reacts to many stimuli of which the ego is completely unaware.
quotes.translation: 换言之，自我并不熟悉可能性的自己，但潜意识的某些部分却了然于心。因为潜意识与可能性的自己一样，知晓自身在倒置时间系统中的存在。潜意识认知到许多未被自我视为实际物理事件的实相，并对自我完全无觉知的众多刺激产生反应。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: However, with the ego at rest the individual may allow communications and dream constructions through, past the ego barrier, in such a manner that he becomes in some ways free. If for example his present expectations are faulty, when the ego rests he may recreate a time when expectations were high. The resulting dream will then partially break the circle of poor expectations, with their shoddy physical constructions, and start such an individual along a more beneficial path. In other words, such a dream may begin to transform the physical environment through lifting inner expectation.
quotes.translation: 然而，当自我处于休息状态时，个体可能允许通讯和梦境建构通过自我的屏障，以某种方式获得自由。例如，如果他当前的期望有误，当自我休息时，他可能重新创造期望高涨的时刻。由此产生的梦境将部分打破不良期望的循环（及其拙劣的物质建构），并引导个体走向更有利的道路。换言之，这样的梦境可能通过提升内在期望开始转变物质环境。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The ego considers itself the self and considers anything outside of its self as being either no self or another such separate identity, and so the individual man is led to believe that telepathy is basically a communication between two or more basically alone, separate and aloof selves. Telepathy does not operate at the level of the ego, although its actions may protrude into the domain of the ego. Telepathy operates within the inner self, within various levels, different levels of the subconscious, where the ideas of separation and limitations of self are not nearly so limiting.
quotes.translation: 自我视自身为全部自我，并将自身之外的一切视为非我或另一个独立的身份，因此个体被引导相信心电感应本质上是两个或多个本质上孤独、分离且疏离的自我之间的沟通。心电感应并不在自我层面运作，尽管其作用可能渗入自我的领域。心电感应运作于内在自我之中，运作于潜意识的不同层次，在那里，关于自我分离与限制的观念远没有那么具有限制性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The ego must sit lightly, otherwise it can smother the talent that lies beneath. This is very properly your talent, your talent as a personality at this particular time. However, you are also more than you know. Your subconscious is a greater part of you than you know, and the ego is nothing more than the topmost portion of the subconscious. There is after all no dividing line, and you are as much your subconscious as you are your ego, and more.
quotes.translation: 自我必须保持轻盈，否则它可能窒息潜藏其下的天赋。这恰是你在此特定时期作为人格的天赋。然而，你所是的也远超过你所知的。你的潜意识比你意识到的更为宏大，而自我不过是潜意识最顶端的部分。毕竟没有分界线，你既是你的潜意识，也是你的自我，甚至更多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Your clock time as you know is camouflage, and does not exist as far as the inner ego is concerned. This certainly should not be strange to you, since you know that even your present personalities can to some extent escape from clock time through the use of psychological time. I have said that there is no time without barriers. The inner ego is aware of fewer barriers and therefore is not bound by time to the degree that the outer ego is bound.
quotes.translation: 你们所知的钟表时间是一种伪装，就内在自我而言它并不存在。这对你们而言当然不应感到陌生，因为你们知道即使是你们当前的人格，也能通过运用心理时间在某种程度上摆脱钟表时间。我曾说过没有障碍就没有时间。内在自我觉察到的障碍较少，因此不像外在自我那样受时间束缚。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: This is extremely difficult, I should imagine. Nevertheless, even when an inner self has sent out a new outer ego upon a new camouflage venture, the previous ego is still afforded an almost unlimited avenue for development. There are many possibilities for it. It can choose to remain what it is, one ego. It will then remain in a somewhat subordinate position to the inner self, but in no more of a subordinate position than it was earlier.
quotes.translation: 我想这极为难以理解。然而，即使当一个内在自我已派出一个新的外在自我进行新的伪装冒险时，先前的自我仍被赋予几乎无限的发展途径。它有许多可能性。它可以选择保持原样，即一个自我。它随后将处于相对于内在自我的从属地位，但不会比它先前所处的从属地位更甚。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The ego may assimilate only a part of a given experience. Sometimes it will not assimilate or accept an experience at all. Remember here again that there is a difference between the ego and consciousness of self. It is not necessary that the ego assimilate all experiences that are open to consciousness of self. ego must have at hand, however, those experiences that are significant for manipulation within the physical environment.
quotes.translation: 自我可能只吸收特定经验的一部分。有时它根本不会吸收或接受某个经验。请再次记住，自我与自我意识之间存在差异。自我不必吸收所有对自我意识开放的经验，但它必须掌握那些在物质环境中具有重要操控意义的经验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The nonphysical personality does not think in terms of words, but experiences concepts in a much more direct manner. This sort of thing simply could not be understood by the physically focused individual ... The survival personality's inner self gives this reassembled ego ideas in the same way that, often, the subconscious gives the ego concepts in physical existence. This reassembled ego then attempts to perceive these insights in terms of sense perceptions, which are sent to the physical individual at the other end. Sometimes the communications are made directly, though they must be sifted through the subconscious of the one who is physical. When that person is trained along these lines, however [as Ruburt is], he or she helps in this process, and a psychological framework, like a bridge,22 is erected that serves to connect the two personalities.
quotes.translation: 非肉体人格不以文字思考，而是以更直接的方式体验概念。这种情形对聚焦于物质世界的个体而言根本无法理解……存续人格的内在自我以同样的方式给予重组后的自我观念——就像在物质存在中，潜意识常将概念传递给自我一样。这个重组后的自我随后试图以感官知觉的方式理解这些洞见，并将其传送给另一端的物质个体。有时这些沟通是直接进行的，但必须通过物质个体的潜意识过滤。然而，当此人经过此类训练时（如鲁柏的情况），他或她会协助这个过程，并建立起如同桥梁般的心理架构，用以连接这两个人格。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The inner ego is fully conscious. It is a portion of you, however, that deals with the formation of events, that glories in a rather rambunctious and creative activity that your specifications of time and place physically preclude. The unconscious, so-called, is -- and I have said this before2 -- quite conscious, but in another realm of activity. There must be a psychological chamber between these two portions of the self, however -- these seemingly undifferentiated areas, in which back-and-forth translations can occur. Dream periods provide that service, of course, so that in dreams the two egos can meet and merge to some extent, comparing notes like strangers who perhaps meet on a train at night, and are amazed to discover, after some conversation, that they are indeed close relatives, each embarked upon the same journey though seemingly they travelled alone.
quotes.translation: 内在自我是完全有意识的。然而，它是你的一部分，负责处理事件的成形，在你们物质时空规范所排除的、相当狂放而富有创造性的活动中欢欣鼓舞。所谓的潜意识——我先前说过——是完全有意识的，但处于另一个活动领域。不过，在这两个自我部分之间——这些看似未分化的领域——必须存在一个心理隔间，使来回的转译得以发生。梦境时段自然提供了这种服务，因此在梦中，两个自我能够相遇并在某种程度上融合，如同两个陌生人在夜班火车上相遇，经过交谈后惊讶地发现他们竟是近亲，各自踏上看似孤独实则相同的旅程。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The communication between the inner and outer egos should obviously be as clear and open as possible. As a general rule, the inner ego depends upon your assessment of physical events. Your involvement in the private aspects of your living, and your participation in mass events, has much to do with your estimation of the physical situation, and with your beliefs and desires regarding it. Give us a moment ... A very simple example (colon): If you want to write a letter you do so. There is no conflict between your desires, beliefs, and the execution of the act, so the action itself flows smoothly. If for some reason or another, through a poor assessment of your reality, you believe that such an act is dangerous, then you will hamper the flow between the desire and the execution. The flow of creativity begun by the inner ego will be impeded.
quotes.translation: 内在自我与外在自我之间的沟通显然应尽可能清晰开放。一般而言，内在自我依赖于你对物质事件的评估。你对自己生活私密层面的投入，以及你对群体事件的参与，都与你对物质情境的评估、以及你对此的信念与欲望密切相关。请稍等片刻……举个极简单的例子（冒号）：如果你想写一封信，你便会付诸行动。你的欲望、信念与行动执行之间毫无冲突，因此行动本身流畅无阻。若因某种原因——因你对自身实相的错误评估——你相信此类行动存在危险，那么你便会阻碍欲望与执行之间的流动。由内在自我启动的创造力之流便会受阻。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: These dreams go unrecognized by the conscious ego; but they do not go unrecorded by the inner self, and they therefore exist, and they form electromagnetic channels of their own to which--make that by which--the physical body itself is affected. The physical organism is constantly changed by all stimuli, whether or not the ego is aware of the stimuli. And it is affected by stimuli, whether or not science realizes that the particular stimuli even exists. Therefore, the physical organism reacts to this continuous unconscious Mental activity in the form of continuing dream experiences, whether or not the individual is awake or asleep. All of these realities go into the formation of the personality, and unless they are all understood the personality itself will remain a mystery.
quotes.translation: 这些梦境未被意识自我所识别；但它们并未被内在自我所忽略，因此它们确实存在，并形成自身的电磁通道——借此——物质身体本身会受到影响。无论自我是否意识到这些刺激，物质有机体始终被所有刺激所改变。并且无论科学是否承认特定刺激的存在，它都会受到刺激的影响。因此，无论个体是清醒还是沉睡，物质有机体都会以持续梦境体验的形式，对这种持续的无意识心智活动做出反应。所有这些实相都参与人格的形成，除非它们全部被理解，否则人格本身将始终是个谜。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: These dream activities have a pulsing quality that could be compared with the pulsing of heartbeat, and that are quite as vital to the human system. So much telepathic communication is received at this level that the lines of individuality are not as brilliant. Yet individuality does exist here, but it takes a strong organizing system, with amazing powers of discrimination, to handle such data; and it takes the human personality to stand firm upon its own identity while it is, on this other dreaming level, open to so many communications that are not its own. It is the ego, practically speaking, who attempts to do the distinguishing here; but the inner core of the self, the inner ego of which we have spoken, manages the basic chore. The setting up of the ego represented at once the necessity of boundaries, represented a cutting apart from, a divorcing, and a rigid limiting function. Initially this was necessary while this new sort of creature learned to maintain itself as a separate unit.
quotes.translation: 这些梦境活动具有一种脉动特质，可与心跳的脉动相类比，且对人类系统同样至关重要。在此层面接收到的如此多的心灵感应沟通，使得个体性的界限不再鲜明。然而个体性确实在此存在，但需要强大的组织系统与惊人的辨别能力来处理此类数据；并且需要人类人格在自身身份上保持坚定，同时在此另一梦境层面，向众多非自身所有的沟通保持开放。实际上，是自我在此试图进行区分；但我们所提及的自我内在核心——内在自我——则负责处理基本任务。自我的设立同时代表了界限的必要性，代表了一种分离、割裂与严格限制的功能。最初，当这种新型生物学习维持自身作为独立单元时，这是必要的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: He tries at times to use a schedule, a disciplined schedule as a fence against the subconscious, and becomes panic-stricken imagining himself without it. This is not to say that a schedule in itself is not good, it is to say that he can and sometimes does misuse one. In these instances the time is actually used to clamp down on the inner self, rather than to release it. These outings will be of benefit here, not only because they allow for spontaneous interaction, but because they serve to break the schedules in ways that do not disturb working hours. They provide a beginning for a habit of release. Here the ego is helping us now, for it is sticking to these plans, and the plans are those the ego itself enjoys, and can accept. He has been afraid to simply sit and think, for fear of letting the spontaneous self show without the symbolized writing mechanism.
quotes.translation: 他有时试图用时间表、一套严格的时间表作为抵御潜意识的栅栏，一旦想象自己失去它便惊慌失措。这并非说时间表本身不好，而是说他可能且有时确实误用了它。在这些情况下，时间实际上被用来压制内在自我，而非释放它。这些外出活动将在此处带来益处，不仅因为它们允许自发互动，更因为它们能以不干扰工作时间的方式打破时间表。它们为养成释放的习惯提供了开端。此时自我正在帮助我们，因为它坚持执行这些计划，而这些计划正是自我本身所享受且能接受的。他过去害怕单纯静坐思考，唯恐让自发自我在没有象征性书写机制的情况下显现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: This sort of experience will always be shielded from the outer ego of necessity. Even a watered-down version of a direct inner experience is a shock to the outer ego on your plane, since the ego imagines itself and its own perceptions to be supreme. You have no idea, even with what training you have, of how shattering such a complete experience would be to the outer ego, so we will take one inner-sense experience at a time.
quotes.translation: 这类体验必然会被外层自我所屏蔽。即便经过稀释的直接内在体验版本，对你们层面的外层自我而言也是一种冲击，因为自我认为自身及其感知至高无上。即便以你们现有的训练，也无法想象如此完整的体验对外层自我会造成何等震撼，因此我们每次只处理一种内在感官体验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The inner ego has experienced, then, objectivity, and has returned to a subjective state. It is a relative storehouse of energy, and it is capable of aiding the outer ego when certain conditions arise. The inner ego may be termed the unfamiliar "I". In many cases it is the I who dreams, bringing valuable information to the personal subconscious, information that may be then used for the benefit of the outer ego itself.
quotes.translation: 内在自我已然体验过客观性，并回归到主观状态。它是一个相对的能量储存库，当特定条件出现时，能够协助外层自我。内在自我可被称为陌生的"我"。在许多情况下，正是这个"我"在梦中带来宝贵信息给个人潜意识，这些信息随后可被用于外层自我自身的利益。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The ego chooses channels of reception with great discrimination; and, again, it censors anything which it feels is a threat to its own dominance. In sleep however many dreams are of a telepathic nature, with strong clairvoyant overtones. It is the ego's persistent discrimination in choosing the stimuli to which it will react that in a large measure determines the nature of physical time as it appears to the personality. The ego, because of its function and basic characteristics, cannot make swift decisions as can the intuitive self. Therefore it perceives events in a peculiar manner, almost in slow motion, so that the whole effect is of a series of separate events, one happening before or after the other.
quotes.translation: 自我以极大的辨别力选择接收的通道，并再次审查任何它认为威胁其主导地位的事物。然而在睡眠中，许多梦境具有心灵感应的性质，并带有强烈的千里眼色彩。正是自我在选择它要回应的刺激时持续不断的辨别力，在很大程度上决定了物理时间在人格面前显现的性质。由于其功能和基本特性，自我无法像直觉自我那样做出快速决定。因此它以一种特殊的方式感知事件，几乎是慢动作，以至于整体效果是一系列独立的事件，一个发生在另一个之前或之后。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: He projected a meaninglessness upon reality, and then fell prey to it. Here the senses themselves can help revive him. (Long pause.) He seems now to be choosing between identities, but there is one waiting for him, and it is a probable ego that he did not adopt as a child. He has been plunged back into a physical universe that he would not accept even as a valid mirage. This time he will be able to find himself within it. (Long pause.) In his case he must accept himself as a human being before he can hope to discover the inner self. The ego got no help from him, and it could not carry on alone. There was a definite splitting of personality elements, and a complete abandonment by the inner self of the ego.
quotes.translation: 他将无意义投射到实相上，然后成为其牺牲品。在这里，感官本身可以帮助他复苏。（长停顿）他现在似乎在选择身份之间，但有一个身份在等待着他，那是他童年时未曾采纳的一个可能性自我。他被抛回一个他连作为有效幻象都不愿接受的物质宇宙。这一次他将能够在其间找到自己。（长停顿）在他的案例中，他必须先接受自己作为人类，才有望发现内在自我。自我没有得到他的帮助，无法独自运作。人格元素出现了明确的分裂，内在自我完全抛弃了自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: The strength of the ego actually depends on the flexibility with which it can accept and assimilate ever more complex actions, and give them a unity of its own. An action or emotion not accepted by the ego, but nevertheless a part of it, will always drain energy from the main core of the ego, despite the ego's denial, and energy that cannot therefore be used by the ego for the purposes of its own purposeful action.
quotes.translation: 自我的力量实际上取决于它接受和同化日益复杂行动并赋予其自身统一性的灵活性。一个不被自我接受但仍是其一部分的行动或情绪，总会从自我的核心中消耗能量，尽管自我否认，而这些能量因此无法被自我用于其有目的的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: But ego's seeming independence from action is basically meaningless, since ego is also action, and can never be otherwise. Any such separation of action from itself only adds to the totality of action, in that it increases actions ability to perceive itself from as many viewpoints as possible. Perspectives represent action's action upon itself. Any one dimension must result in another dimension, for the action within any given dimension can never complete itself, but will continue.
quotes.translation: 但自我看似独立于行动的特性，本质上毫无意义，因为自我本身就是行动，且永远无法成为其他形态。任何将行动与自身分离的举动，只会增加行动的整体性，因为它提升了行动从尽可能多的视角感知自身的能力。视角代表行动对自身的作用。任何单一维度必然衍生出另一维度，因为既定维度内的行动永远无法自我完成，而将持续延展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Nevertheless, the ego is the tool by which the hidden self manipulates in the physical universe as you know it. The ego enables you to use or focus your artistic ability along the lines necessary to make it effective on your plane. However when the ego becomes involved with fears to a greater or lesser extent, it ceases to be an effective tool and becomes instead a hammer hitting you incessantly over the head.
quotes.translation: 然而，自我是隐藏的自我在你们所知的物质宇宙中运作的工具。自我使你得以将艺术才能聚焦于必要方向，使其在你们层面产生实效。但当自我或多或少陷入恐惧时，它便不再是有效工具，反而成为不断敲击你头顶的锤子。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Humanity's so-called supreme blossom is the human ego, and this is at times a poisoning blossom indeed. As I have said before, there is nothing wrong with the ego. The point however remains that man became so fascinated with the conscious ego that he ignored the part of himself that made the ego possible, and ignored the part of himself that gives to the ego the very powers of which he is so consciously proud.
quotes.translation: 人类所谓最璀璨的花朵是人类的自我，而这有时确实是朵毒花。如我先前所述，自我本身并无过错。但关键在于，人类如此沉迷于意识自我，以致忽略了使自我得以存在的自身部分，也忽略了赋予自我那些令其引以为傲之能力的自身部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The habitual pattern or characteristic nature of the ego may then be led to refuse to accept an emotion, at the same time that a pattern has already been set to receive the particular type of emotion. Here the ego fights against itself. Such an emotion may of course be given release through dreams, but this is of limited value to the ego involved, since the ego does not accept the reality of dream existence.
quotes.translation: 自我惯常的模式或特性，可能会因此拒绝接纳某种情绪，同时却已建立起接收特定类型情绪的模式。此时自我正与自己对抗。这类情绪固然可通过梦境释放，但对涉及其中的自我而言价值有限，因为自我并不承认梦境存在的真实性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The confident inner self will let the ego manipulate in the physical world, but will not allow it to become fiercely overprotective. Your work contains the strength of your inner self in many ways. Your particular ego's function is to show this work to the world as you know it. I hesitate, and I mean this, to offer practical advice to one who tries to be so practical. But my dear Joseph, there is no true practicality in smothering your abilities by working in a position where you cannot use your abilities. You will not be paid for abilities you cannot use, since I know you must think in financial terms. Your ego's job is to help you trade your true abilities for your daily bread.
quotes.translation: 自信的内在自我会让自我在物质世界中运作，但不会允许它变得过度保护。你的作品以多种方式承载着内在自我的力量。你特定自我的功能，就是将这作品展示给你所认知的世界。我犹豫着——且此言非虚——要给一位如此务实的人提供实际建议。但亲爱的约瑟夫，若因从事无法发挥才能的职位而压抑天赋，这绝非真正的务实。既然我知道你不得不考虑经济问题，那么无法施展的才能自然无法为你带来报酬。你自我的职责，正是协助你用真实才能换取每日的面包。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: It is therefore with the second alternating group of characteristics that most such problems arise. An ego who can, and has at one time or another accepted as part of itself a violent and unruly desire to kill, for example, will not automatically reject the emotion of hatred. He may dislike it, but he will recognize it as a part of himself during whatever period it is assimilated. An ego which once accepted such an idea of violence, and knew it as a possibility of action, such an ego, if he then rejects the conception, can no longer afford, ever, to recognize this once acceptable emotion, for he is only too aware of the action that could have at one time developed.
quotes.translation: 因此，大多数此类问题都源于第二组交替出现的特性。例如，一个曾经接纳过暴力且难以抑制的杀戮欲望作为自身一部分的自我，不会自动排斥憎恨这种情绪。他或许会厌恶它，但在其被同化的任何阶段，都会将其视为自身的一部分。一个曾接纳过这种暴力概念、并知晓其可能转化为行动的自我——倘若他后来拒绝这种认知，便再也无法承认这种曾可接受的感受，因为他太清楚这种情绪曾可能发展出的行动了。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: It must, of course, succeed to some extent, or the physically oriented personality as you know it would not exist. The ego is of course a part of action. There are endless levels of action however, which the ego does not perceive. It is possible for the inner and outer egos to merge to some extent, and this merging when it occurs does indeed represent the formation of a new kind of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 自我当然必须在某种程度上获得成功，否则你所知的物质导向人格便不会存在。自我自然是行动的一部分。然而，存在着自我无法感知的无穷无尽的活动层次。内在自我与外在自我有可能在某种程度上融合，而这种融合发生时，确实代表着一种新型意识的形成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: In many cases he perceives in a dream a future physical event, and then within the dream situation acts out various possible solutions, until he hits upon the most agreeable one. In the dream state the personality actually has at its command a stupendous amount of subconscious information of which the ego is not aware. It is actually more practical therefore to seek the solution in the dreaming state, or in periods of dissociation if you prefer. The amount of data available to the subconscious is simply superior in quality and larger in quantity to that available to the ego, and this information can be used effectively through suggestion.
quotes.translation: 在许多情况下，他在梦中感知到未来的物质事件，然后在梦境情境中演练各种可能的解决方案，直到找到最合意的那一个。在梦境状态中，人格实际上掌握着大量自我未曾察觉的潜意识信息。因此，在梦境状态或你更偏好的解离时期寻求解决方案，实际上更为实际。潜意识可用的数据在质量上优于自我，在数量上也多于自我，这些信息可以通过暗示有效运用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: His abilities are considerable in the healing line, and again for this reason it is extremely important that he is understanding of himself as well as others. His ego is (underlined) a kind one, but the structure of any ego is such that it considers, or can consider easily, psychic ability as a sign of its own power; or feel possessive of the ability, and overly proud of it, even when it knows it originates in other layers of the self. We want our friend to have a strong and healthy overall personality balance, for this will allow him to use his abilities well, and then the abilities themselves will further add to his own development.
quotes.translation: 他在疗愈方面能力出众，正因如此，他对自己和他人的理解至关重要。他的自我（强调）是仁慈的，但任何自我的结构都会倾向于将通灵能力视为自身力量的标志；或对能力产生占有欲，并过分引以为傲，即便它知道能力源自自我的其他层面。我们希望我们的朋友拥有强大而健康的整体人格平衡，这将使他能够善用自身能力，而这些能力本身也将进一步促进他的成长。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Since suggestion is used constantly, and by every cell in your body, then it is not a question of whether or not you can use suggestion, but of how to use it more effectively. And this will involve a balanced personality, for in such a personality action will be allowed freedom of expression. The ego will gain and not lose in the process, but we will not have an autocratic and willful ego, but a reasonable and even disciplined ego that is aware of its position, aware of its own dependency, as well as of its own peculiar and unique perceptive apartness. For the apartness is dependent upon its being a part of the whole gestalt.
